title: feat(core): support dynamic filesystem resolution via requestContext in Workspace
summary: Adds support for a dynamic filesystem resolver function in Workspace config, matching the pattern already used by skills, instructions, model, tools, and memory. The filesystem option now accepts either a static WorkspaceFilesystem instance (existing behavior) or a resolver function `({ requestContext }) => WorkspaceFilesystem` that resolves per-request. Changes span: new WorkspaceFilesystemResolver type, hasFilesystemConfig()/resolveFilesystem() public methods on Workspace, Proxy-based dynamic resolution in workspace tools at execution time, class constructor guard, read-only enforcement for dynamically resolved filesystems, comprehensive tests (workspace unit tests + tool integration tests), documentation updates, and a changeset.
maintainerNotes: Well-structured community PR that addresses issue #13133 (from Discord). Maintainer NikAiyer has engaged twice asking the author to fix merge conflicts. The PR currently has merge conflicts that need resolving. CI checks are stuck in PENDING state (Core, E2E, MCP, Memory, RAG, Combined store tests never ran — likely blocked by the conflicts or missing secrets for external contributors). CodeRabbit review flagged only minor nitpicks (docs parameter naming, example input validation). The Proxy-based approach in tools.ts for injecting the resolved filesystem is clever but warrants careful review — it creates a proxy workspace object on every tool execution when a resolver is configured. The class constructor detection via regex on Function.prototype.toString is a nice defensive touch but may not work with transpiled/minified code. No human maintainer has reviewed the actual code yet.
